message:
fix(runtime+conformance+server): clear 5 clippy lints + unblock drift exposed by fr-command green (br-frankenredis-ea1j)

Resolves the workspace clippy -D warnings gate. The five original lints in
fr-runtime/src/lib.rs were:

  1. unused_variables  lib.rs:904   let acl_pubsub_default = self.acl_pubsub_default;
  2. unused_variables  lib.rs:920   (same in add_user)
  3. redundant_closure lib.rs:908   .or_insert_with(|| AclUser::new_default())
  4. redundant_closure lib.rs:924   (same)
  5. ifs_same_cond     lib.rs:1125  dead `resetkeys` arm masked by lib.rs:1016

Fixes applied:
- Drop both dead `acl_pubsub_default` bindings. set_requirepass mutates
  the DEFAULT_AUTH_USER singleton (new_default is correct); add_user is
  a test harness that immediately overwrites passwords and keeps full
  permissions for the created user (new_default is correct there too).
  If future work wants to honor pubsub_default for add_user, call
  AclUser::new_restricted(acl_pubsub_default) explicitly.
- Swap both `.or_insert_with(|| AclUser::new_default())` closures to
  `.or_insert_with(AclUser::new_default)`.
- Delete the duplicate `resetkeys` arm at the bottom of the SETUSER
  modifier chain (the first arm at lib.rs:1016 always fires; the bodies
  are identical so no semantic change).

Cascade cleanup unblocked by the fr-command green gate (frankenredis-v4x3
and frankenredis-j4vq fixed the fr-command fail-fast; everything below
that point had drifted undetected):

- fr-runtime tests: three SLOWLOG arity assertions expected the old
  "subcommand" suffix; re-pinned to "command" to match upstream.
- fr-conformance fixtures core_acl.json (7 entries) and core_stream.json
  (3 entries): replace "subcommand" with "command" in the error
  expectations so the differential core_acl / core_stream suites
  re-converge.
- fr-conformance lib.rs: CONFIG GET acl* expectation updated to include
  acl-pubsub-default=resetchannels (registered as an acl* key after
  72bf4d2 but the assertion was missed).
- fr-conformance live_oracle_orchestrator: baseline matrix order
  now includes fr_p2c_006_replication_journey (activated by 2000608
  but the expectation wasn't updated).
- fr-conformance user_journey_corpus_gate: active/planned differential
  counts realigned to the live fixture (8 active / 1 planned after
  FR-P2C-008-J001 and one other flipped from planned → active).
- fr-server replica_sync test: ROLE reply 5th element is Integer(-1)
  (not 0) for any replica state other than "connected", matching the
  fr-runtime handler at lib.rs:9437 and upstream Redis convention.

Gate status after this commit:
  cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  → exit 0
  cargo test --workspace --all-targets                   → 99%+ pass;
    two residual failures (tcp_sentinel_failover_integration requires
    --config and tcp_waitaof_appendfsync_no_keeps_local_ack_visible_
    across_bgrewriteaof depends on frankenredis-jqgv WAITAOF stub) are
    orthogonal and will be filed as follow-ups.

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -839,11 +839,16 @@ mod tests {
             .iter()
             .map(|suite| suite.name.as_str())
             .collect::<Vec<_>>();
+        // fr_p2c_006_replication_journey was promoted into the baseline
+        // matrix after commit 2000608 activated the live-oracle replication
+        // differential; this expectation was missed at the time.
+        // (br-frankenredis-ea1j)
         assert_eq!(
             names,
             vec![
                 "core_strings",
                 "fr_p2c_001_eventloop_journey",
+                "fr_p2c_006_replication_journey",
                 "fr_p2c_003_dispatch_journey",
                 "core_errors",
                 "fr_p2c_002_protocol_negative",
